overview.text: The NSW State Emergency Service is the lead combat agency for floods, storms and tsunamis in NSW. It also provides general rescue, vertical/cliff rescue, and is the primary land-based response for severe-weather incidents.

items: Established in 1955 as the State Emergency Service.
Lead agency for storm, flood and tsunami response under the State Emergency and Rescue Management Act.
Capabilities include flood rescue (in-water and boat), road-crash rescue, vertical rescue, urban search and rescue, and community education.
Activations escalate from local Unit through Region to State Operations Centre at Wollongong.
Major events frequently patch SES with FRNSW, RFS, NSW Police and NSW Ambulance on shared liaison talkgroups.
